user_message: Help me build renewal risk for Lucerne Publishing. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-11 11:17 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Lucerne Publishing renewal risk

Our team encountered some challenges with renewal risk. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 10:04 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Lucerne Publishing
Date: 2024-03-22
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Lucerne Publishing)

[00:01:15] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is renewal risk.

[00:06:00] Priya (Lucerne Publishing): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:15:00] Priya (Lucerne Publishing): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:20:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:23:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Lucerne Publishing: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
